item.title: Texas school police pepper-sprayed, tackled and tasered students
item.description: School officers across the state turned to heavy-handed tactics on children, often in response to minor misbehavior, investigation shows.

item.title: Federal court allows Texas immigration law to take effect, continuing legal seesaw
item.dc:creator: Alex Nguyen

item.title: Teaching restrictions prompted half of surveyed Texas Tech faculty to alter courses, results show
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: “The last of the gentlemen Republicans”: John Cornyn’s four-decade political career ends with a MAGA uprising
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: With limited options, Corpus Christi focuses on delaying – not avoiding – its looming water crisis
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: A Texas town hopes a new data center will pay to fix its cracked streets and leaking pipes
item.dc:creator: The Waco Bridge

item.title: ICE sued over “inhumane” conditions at Camp East Montana in West Texas
item.description: The El Paso tent camp has seen at least three detainee deaths, a measles outbreak and nearly 50 detention standards violations in less than a year of operation.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Outside spending blitz defined the close of Texas’ District 35 Democratic runoff
item.description: To counter what appeared to be a GOP push to elevate Maureen Galindo, who’d voiced antisemitic stereotypes, national Democrats carpet-bombed the San Antonio area with $1.7 million worth of ads.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Members of pro-immigration group hit by Trump crackdown on immigration
item.description: La Union Del Pueblo Entero has seen engagement at its 8,000-member organization drop off after the Trump administration’s immigration raids.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Elected Officials Directory
item.description: To help Texans know more about their representatives, we’ve gathered the bios, contact information and more for the occupants of the state’s highest offices.

item.title: Government Salaries Explorer
item.description: Review the compensation of hundreds of thousands of state and municipal employees in Texas.

item.title: Texas Public Schools Explorer
item.description: Review enrollment trends, demographics, teacher experience, pay and outcomes for all schools, and more.

item.title: Accused of a crime after shooting a migrant in Minnesota, ICE agent is arrested in South Texas
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: In Harris County judge runoff, a Democratic upset underscores voters’ desire for new blood
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Texas using DPS records to confirm citizenship of voters flagged by federal database
item.dc:creator: Natalia Contreras

item.title: Recent rain gives two Corpus Christi reservoirs a much-needed boost
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Texas’ app age verification law allowed to go into effect for now

item.dc:creator: Associated Press

item.title: East Texas could be the key to developing critical lithium supply for the U.S. military
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Texas’ discipline push sends kids to ‘jail-like’ campuses
item.dc:creator: The Hechinger Report

item.title: Texas prisoners face new book ban after hundreds test positive for synthetic drugs
